issues.description: Email claims the practice is 'losing 30% of potential patients' but no evidence supports this specific figure.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace '30% of potential patients' with 'after-hours appointment requests' which is supported by the observed phone-only booking evidence.

issues.description: Message says 'I've been following your practice's growth' but there is no evidence of following or prior engagement.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace with 'I came across your practice while researching dental clinics in the area' which is honest and credible.

issues.description: Email ends with 'Let me know your thoughts' which is vague and low-conversion.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace with 'Would it be useful if I sent over the booking review?' — specific and low-friction.

revised_fields.primary_email:
Hi Sarah,

I was looking at Bright Smile Dental's website and noticed your contact page directs patients to call during office hours to book appointments.

For a practice your size, that likely means after-hours appointment requests may slip through.

I put together a short booking flow review for your practice. It flags a couple of things that might be costing you appointments.

Would it be useful if I sent it over?

Best,
Luc
revised_fields.linkedin_message: Hi Sarah — I came across Bright Smile Dental while researching dental practices in the area. Noticed your patient booking is phone-only, which can create friction for after-hours requests. I put together a short booking flow review — happy to share if useful.
